synopsis: The novel opens without committing to any single plotline, instead establishing the modal atmosphere—theological dread, rocket descent, sexuality, waste—that will saturate the narrative. As a hinge episode, 1.01 functions as a threshold: Pirate Prentice's dream of evacuation and the screaming across the sky set the rocket's terminal arc as the novel's governing figure before any character-driven pursuit begins.

Pirate Prentice's banana breakfast and rooftop garden establish the Counterforce line (P06) at full intensity, grounding it not in resistance but in comic domesticity and wartime fantasy-reception. The episode's dominant mode is pop-song comedy, framing survival as absurd ritual beside the rockets rather than opposition to them.

Pointsman's White Visitation programme takes structural hold as the Pavlovian-control plotline (P05) establishes itself through statistical inference and bureaucratic surveillance. Slothrop appears only as a latent correlation—his body already an object of institutional attention before the pursuit begins.

A hinge episode that decisively establishes Slothrop's rocket-body correlation (P01) and locks the Pointsman programme (P05) into its experimental framework. The collision of Pavlovian science and sexual conditioning produces the novel's central entanglement: a body whose desire maps onto rocket impacts, making it both experimental datum and pursued object.

The séance at the White Visitation introduces the occult countercurrent to Pointsman's science, while Roger and Jessica's intimacy (P06) establishes itself at full intensity as fragile life beside the rockets. Control is revealed to operate from within, even as the Counterforce line offers its first alternative: statistical scepticism grounded in tenderness.

Pointsman's programme reaches full structural command as the White Visitation consolidates its Pavlovian apparatus, with sexuality and domination running at maximum alongside scientific control. Roger and Jessica survive beside this machinery at reduced intensity, their intimacy already registering the institutional pressure that will eventually dissolve it.

A concentrated Pointsman episode that sustains the control plotline (P05) at maximum intensity without interruption from other narrative lines. The pairing of science-mode and sexuality-domination at equal peaks reveals the behavioural programme as inseparable from erotic power—the experiment is already a form of command over the body.

Pointsman's programme (P05) shifts from establishment to revelation, exposing the mechanisms of surveillance and bodily conditioning that underpin the rocket correlation. Slothrop recurs at moderate intensity, his presence now shaped by the institutional apparatus rather than his own agency—the body-as-datum is becoming visible.

Roger and Jessica's intimacy (P06) returns to full intensity in a second establishing episode, offering the novel's most sustained register of preterite counter-knowledge: statistical scepticism, songs, and domestic warmth against the Blitz. Pointsman's control recedes to background, creating a structural alternation between institutional surveillance and fugitive tenderness.

Slothrop's rocket-pursuit plotline (P01) reaches a second decisive establishment as his sexual-geographical map becomes the central object of institutional attention. The entanglement of correlation, paranoia, and costume begins in earnest, while Pointsman's programme provides the interpretive framework that converts Slothrop's body into data.

The Pökler/Leni/Ilse/Jamf/Imipolex line (P04) establishes itself at full intensity, introducing the family-loss-as-technical-work nexus through Jamf's chemistry and the Imipolex connection to rocket manufacture. This is the novel's industrial-historical plotline: where family, forced labour, and material invention become structurally inseparable.

The Pointsman programme continues to reveal its mechanisms at moderate intensity while Slothrop persists as background pressure. Science and sexuality modes again peak together, sustaining the Part 1 pattern in which the body is simultaneously experimental object and erotic surface.

Three plotlines converge at low-to-moderate intensity—Slothrop, Pökler, and Pointsman—creating the first episode where the rocket correlation (P01), industrial chemistry (P04), and behavioural control (P05) share structural space. The braid begins to thicken: distinct institutional forces are revealed to share objects and infrastructure.

A hinge episode that establishes the Blicero/Katje/Gottfried plotline (P02) at maximum intensity through the erotic-sacrificial command structure at the V-2 battery. The 00000 rocket emerges as terminal parabola—fairy-tale script fused with launch logic—while the species-extinction register sounds for the first time, linking personal sacrifice to planetary scale.

Slothrop's pursuit plotline (P01) achieves full establishment as correlation, map-reading, and paranoia crystallise into the novel's governing quest structure. Elect-preterite theology enters alongside the sexual and scientific modes, positioning Slothrop's body at the intersection of salvation narrative and conditioning experiment.

Roger and Jessica's Counterforce line (P06) sustains itself at full intensity through the survival function—wartime intimacy persisting against institutional pressure with minimal modal overlay. The episode's sparse texture marks it as a structural breathing point: pure tenderness before the apparatus closes in.

The Slothrop-Pointsman dyad recurs at matched moderate intensity, with science and sexuality again peaking. The pattern has become legible by this point in Part 1: the body under Pavlovian observation is also the body under erotic scrutiny, and the two regimes of knowledge operate through the same institutional channels.

The White Visitation's control programme continues alongside a maximum-intensity surge of occult/divination mode, marking the moment where Pointsman's rationalist apparatus coexists most uncomfortably with the séance and paranormal currents running through the same institution. Science and the occult prove to be rival knowledge systems sharing the same institutional body.

Pökler's plotline (P04) reaches its second full establishment, now explicitly touching forced-labour and Holocaust-adjacency registers for the first time. Waste/residue/body-grotesque peaks at maximum intensity, signalling that the industrial-chemical line carries within it the bodily cost of rocket production—family loss becoming inseparable from the camp system's material logic.

A late Part 1 Pointsman episode sustaining the control programme at moderate intensity through the familiar science-sexuality pairing. The White Visitation apparatus continues to reveal itself, but with diminishing structural novelty—the institution's mechanisms are now fully exposed, preparing for Part 2's shift in operational mode.

Part 1 closes with three plotlines at reduced intensity—Slothrop, Pökler, and the Counterforce—creating a structural diminuendo. The minimal modal presence marks the transition out of London's institutional space, setting the stage for Part 2's pursuit across the Riviera.

A hinge episode that pivots the novel from London's institutional apparatus to the Riviera pursuit: Slothrop's rocket-chasing (P01) shifts from correlation to active pursuit and Pointsman's programme (P05) converts from revelation to operational pursuit of its experimental subject. Blicero's line (P02) recurs at background intensity, reminding the reader that the sacrificial parabola persists beneath the Casino's surface.

Slothrop's pursuit of rocket intelligence intensifies across the Casino Hermann Goering as Katje becomes the visible link between P01 and P02—her role as Pointsman's handler placing her at the intersection of behavioural experiment and erotic-sacrificial history. The triple plotline density sustains the Part 2 pattern of pursuit-within-surveillance.

Slothrop continues his Zone pursuit at maximum intensity through occult and corporate-military channels, with five active modes creating the richest modal texture of Part 2. Pointsman's programme pursues at moderate intensity, its apparatus now operating at distance through agents and controlled information rather than direct institutional confinement.

Pointsman's control programme (P05) reaches its Part 2 peak at maximum intensity while Slothrop recedes to background, producing an episode where the apparatus itself becomes the structural protagonist. The technocratic-continuation register sounds for the first time, signalling that wartime surveillance systems are reorganising for peacetime deployment.

Slothrop's pursuit resumes at full intensity alongside a moderate recurrence of the Pökler/Jamf/Imipolex line (P04), bringing rocket hardware and corporate infrastructure into direct contact with the picaresque pursuit. The corporate-military web reaches maximum single-mode concentration, revealing the pursuit as movement through power structures rather than geography.

A concentrated Slothrop episode at maximum pursuit intensity with the occult-divination mode at moderate intensity, suggesting that rocket pursuit is beginning to operate through paranormal channels—rumour and intuition rather than documentary evidence—foreshadowing the transformation that Part 3 will accelerate.

The two hardware-oriented plotlines—Slothrop's rocket pursuit (P01) and Pökler's industrial-chemical line (P04)—converge at maximum intensity, with P04 shifting from recurrence to revelation. The corporate-military web saturates the episode, making this the structural moment where the rocket's material genealogy becomes fully legible to the pursuing body.

Part 2 closes with Pointsman's control programme (P05) reaching maximum intensity through the function of failure—the behavioural apparatus collapses at the moment of its fullest expression. The structural event is the White Visitation's organisational death, its Pavlovian logic unable to survive the transition from wartime institution to Zone chaos.

A hinge episode that launches Part 3's immense middle section with Slothrop in maximum pursuit mode, crossing into the Zone. The stripped-back modal palette—only language-allusion and corporate-military web—marks the shift from institutional London and Casino Riviera to the Zone's open, fractured geography where pursuit will gradually become scattering.

Slothrop's pursuit continues at full intensity but the historical recurrence layer activates three registers simultaneously—forced labour, Holocaust adjacency, and technocratic continuation—making this the first episode where the Zone's rocket geography is explicitly layered with extermination history. Pursuit now means movement through a landscape of industrial death.

The Enzian/Schwarzkommando plotline (P03) establishes itself at maximum intensity through the colonial-genocide register at its highest level, introducing the novel's most sustained counter-line: Herero survivors inheriting the coloniser's weapon while debating whether assembly of the 00001 rocket constitutes survival or self-erasure. The episode anchors everything that follows in Part 3's colonial-historical dimension.

Three plotlines share structural space for the first time in Part 3—Slothrop's pursuit, Enzian's establishment, and Tchitcherine's Soviet counterline—braiding the rocket's meaning across American, Herero, and Soviet interpretive frameworks. The occult mode suggests that the Zone's multiple pursuits are guided by divination and coincidence as much as intelligence.

Tchitcherine's Soviet counterline (P07) takes structural command at full pursuit intensity, with the Schwarzkommando surviving at background level—the half-brothers' lines running parallel without recognition. The Central Asian analepsis deepens the Soviet bureaucratic apparatus as a rival system of rocket-knowledge, one that blocks kinship through institutional classification.

A pivotal episode where Slothrop's thread function shifts from 'pursues' to 'transforms'—the Rocketman costume marking the moment when identity begins dissolving into staged performance. The film/body/image line (P08) recurs alongside this transformation, and the five-mode density makes this one of Part 3's most texturally complex passages.

Slothrop's transformation continues at maximum intensity as the Rocketman identity deepens through the Neubabelsberg film-studio environment. The pop-comedy mode reframes the costume not as heroic disguise but as absurd performance—identity is becoming a surface that the Zone's systems can inscribe upon.

The film/body/image plotline (P08) reaches maximum intensity in its purest form—an episode governed entirely by the logic of film, staged bodies, and mediated performance without any other plotline competing for structural command. Image becomes the episode's entire substance.

Tchitcherine's pursuit line (P07) dominates at full intensity while Slothrop recedes to background, marking a structural handoff between the novel's two primary pursuers. The minimal modal presence makes this a clean pursuit episode—Soviet intelligence chasing rocket secrets through the Zone's corporate-military web.

Slothrop's pursuit returns to maximum intensity alongside background traces of Tchitcherine and the film-body line, creating a three-strand braid at asymmetric intensities. The film-media mode sustains the Part 3 pattern where pursuit and performance have become difficult to distinguish.

The novel's most historically weighted hinge episode: Pökler's plotline (P04) reaches maximum intensity through the mourning function as he arrives at the Dora/Mittelwerk camp, where forced-labour and technocratic-continuation registers peak simultaneously at their highest levels. The episode's total intensity of 23, tied with 4.12, marks it as one of the novel's two structural peaks—the point where industrial complicity and camp infrastructure become inescapable.

Slothrop's pursuit resumes after the Pökler-Dora hinge, with film-media and corporate-military modes sustaining the Zone's pervasive overlay of performance and institutional power. The background film-body recurrence keeps the image-line visible without allowing it to compete with the rocket pursuit's structural priority.

Pökler's line (P04) achieves its revelation function at maximum intensity—the full scope of industrial complicity becoming legible—while the Schwarzkommando (P03) survives alongside it, creating a structural parallel between German-industrial and colonial-survivor perspectives on the same rocket infrastructure. Four recurrence registers activate simultaneously, the highest density in the novel.

The film/body/image plotline (P08) takes structural command at maximum intensity aboard the Anubis, with Greta Erdmann's presence converting the ship into a floating stage where bodies become surfaces and testimony. Slothrop pursues at moderate intensity but is increasingly absorbed into the film-body logic rather than driving the narrative forward.

The film-body plotline (P08) shifts from recurrence to transformation at maximum intensity, marking the Anubis episodes as the site where mediated identity begins actively reshaping the narrative's terms. Slothrop fades to background, his pursuit now subordinate to the image-logic governing the ship's enclosed world.

Three plotlines share space at asymmetric intensities as the film-body transformation (P08) continues to dominate, with Blicero's line (P02) and Slothrop's pursuit providing background pressure. Sexuality/domination peaks at maximum, binding the Anubis's erotic performances to the sacrificial and rocket-command registers that link P08 to P02.

Two plotlines reach maximum intensity simultaneously: Blicero's line (P02) shifts to convergence as the sacrificial rocket narrative tightens, while the film-body plotline (P08) sustains its transformation. The sexuality/domination peak fuses erotic command with rocket logic, making the Anubis the structural junction where fairy-tale sacrifice and mediated performance become indistinguishable.

The film-body plotline (P08) sustains maximum intensity through recurrence, extending the Anubis sequence's mediated world into a self-contained filmic episode. Slothrop's pursuit persists only as a background thread, confirming that in this stretch of Part 3 the novel's structural centre has shifted from rocket-chasing to image-logic.

Slothrop's pursuit returns to maximum intensity as a solo plotline, reasserting the rocket quest after the Anubis interlude. The comedy, film, and corporate-military modes create a Zone-transit texture where pursuit operates through pop-cultural surfaces and institutional debris rather than direct technical investigation.

A stripped-back pursuit episode with Slothrop at maximum intensity and only the corporate-military web as modal context. The minimal texture marks this as pure Zone transit—a structural clearance before the scattering episodes that will dissolve the pursuit entirely.

The thread function 'scatters' appears in Slothrop's line for the first time, marking the beginning of his dissolution as a coherent protagonist. Enzian's Schwarzkommando (P03) takes structural command at maximum survival intensity as colonial-genocide recurrence peaks, establishing the colonial-historical line as the narrative's load-bearing structure while Slothrop fragments.

A hinge episode where the Schwarzkommando plotline (P03) achieves its revelation function at maximum intensity: the full historical weight of colonial genocide, species extinction, and counter-text becomes structurally legible. Five modes activate simultaneously—comedy, language, occult, corporate, theology—creating the richest modal density since the novel's opening, while Slothrop continues to scatter at the margins.

The Counterforce line (P06) resurfaces at maximum intensity through the survival function as the comedy-song mode provides its primary register. After a long absence through the middle of Part 3, this episode reminds the reader that preterite resistance—local, comic, organisational rather than heroic—persists alongside the scattering and colonial-historical lines.

The Counterforce (P06) sustains full survival intensity in a second consecutive episode, thickened by film-performance and sexuality modes alongside the comedy register. The doubled P06 presence marks the Counterforce's brief period of organisational visibility before Part 4 will convert survival into failure.

Slothrop's scattering retreats into American analepsis and Puritan ancestry, with language-allusion and elect-preterite theology peaking at maximum intensity simultaneously. The scattering is not only geographical but genealogical, dissolving the protagonist backward through his own family's theological history.

Slothrop's scattering paradoxically reaches maximum intensity—the dissolution itself becoming structurally decisive—while the Schwarzkommando survives at moderate level alongside him. The corporate-military web provides the only modal context, suggesting that the scattering happens within the Zone's power structures rather than escaping them.

Enzian's survival and Tchitcherine's pursuit run at matched moderate intensities, the half-brothers' lines braiding without recognition. The corporate-military mode binds both plotlines to the same institutional infrastructure—they share the Zone but not knowledge of each other, kinship negated by bureaucratic and colonial classification.

Slothrop's scattering reaches a second maximum-intensity peak through the Zone's dispersed geography, with comedy and corporate-military modes providing the structural context for his dissolution. Elect-preterite theology appears at background intensity, a fading register of the salvation narrative that Slothrop's body once seemed to carry.

A hinge episode where Pökler's mourning (P04) reaches maximum intensity for the final time—the family-industrial plotline's terminal statement as his complicity with the Dora camp system becomes irrevocable. Slothrop fades to background as the scattering gives way to the novel's most sustained confrontation between individual engineering memory and the forced-labour infrastructure it served.

Slothrop's scattering reaches its lowest point—intensity 1, barely registering in the narrative—while occult and theological modes persist at moderate levels. The episode inhabits the American analeptic space where the Puritan ancestry's elect-preterite logic continues to radiate even as its protagonist dissolves.

A minimal late-Part-3 episode with Slothrop scattering at moderate intensity through the comedy-song mode alone. The structural sparseness—lowest total intensity in Part 3—enacts the dissolution it describes: the braid has thinned to a single strand at half-strength, carried only by the preterite register of pop-song and joke.

Part 3 closes with Slothrop at background intensity and Tchitcherine's pursuit taking structural priority, pointing toward Part 4's resolution of the Soviet counterline. The minimal modal presence marks the transition from the Zone's immense middle to the novel's terminal compression.

Part 4 opens with three plotlines at reduced intensities—Slothrop scattering, the Counterforce beginning to fail, and the film-body line persisting as afterimage. The comedy and corporate-military modes set the terminal register: resistance will be absorbed into the system it opposed, and the comic mode that once sustained alternatives is now the medium of that absorption.

The Counterforce plotline (P06) reaches maximum intensity through the function of failure—the novel's most concentrated demonstration that preterite resistance cannot survive the corporate-military web's structural dominance, which peaks at intensity 3. Slothrop scatters at background level, his dissolution now parallel to the Counterforce's organisational collapse.

The Counterforce line fades to background intensity as its failure becomes a settled condition rather than a dramatic event. Elect-preterite theology and waste/body-grotesque modes persist at moderate levels, marking the aftermath: what survives the Counterforce's collapse is not resistance but the theological and bodily residue of its impossibility.

The densest plotline episode in Part 4, with four simultaneous lines: the Schwarzkommando (P03) achieves convergence at maximum intensity alongside colonial-genocide recurrence at peak level, while Blicero's sacrificial line, Slothrop's scattering, and the Counterforce's failure create the structural backdrop. The 00001 rocket assembly approaches resolution as the colonial-historical line delivers its terminal statement.

A hinge episode of enormous structural weight where the Blicero/Gottfried plotline (P02) reaches its sacrificial terminus—the 00000 rocket fired with Gottfried inside the Imipolex shroud. Three modes peak simultaneously at maximum (corporate-military, terminal-rocket-descent, sexuality-domination), producing the novel's single most concentrated fusion of erotic command, technological infrastructure, and death.

Slothrop's scattering reaches a final maximum-intensity peak—the most structurally decisive statement of his dissolution—as elect-preterite theology surges to its highest level in the novel, reframing the scattered protagonist within the deepest register of Puritan damnation and preterite persistence. No human-scale resistance survives the scattering.

Tchitcherine's plotline (P07) achieves its transformation function at maximum intensity—the Soviet pursuit line dissolving through Geli Tripping's organic counter-magic into unrecognition rather than confrontation. The half-brothers' meeting is structured by the failure of recognition: kinship negated by bureaucratic and colonial classification.

The Counterforce plotline (P06) reaches its final maximum intensity through the mourning function—Roger Mexico's grief marking the line's shift from organisational failure to personal loss. Comedy, language, corporate-military, and theology modes sustain the episode's density, creating a register where mourning and comedy coexist as the preterite's terminal condition.

Three plotlines converge as Blicero's sacrificial line (P02) sustains maximum intensity while the Counterforce (P06) shifts to parody—resistance surviving only as the comic inversion of what it once attempted. The five-mode density and sexuality/domination at peak intensity make this one of Part 4's most structurally loaded episodes, where sacrifice, parody, and pursuit coexist within the same rocket-infrastructure space.

The Schwarzkommando convergence (P03) reaches maximum intensity as the 00001 rocket's assembly enters its terminal phase, with colonial-genocide recurrence at peak level and terminal-rocket-descent at maximum. The parallel with Blicero's sacrifice at moderate intensity creates the novel's most explicit structural pairing: 00000 and 00001, colonial extermination and colonial survival, two parabolas aimed at the same sky.

Blicero's line and Tchitcherine's pursuit run at matched moderate intensities in a penultimate episode whose symmetry enacts the structural parallels running throughout—both lines shaped by terminal-rocket-descent and sexuality-domination modes, both carrying colonial and state-power histories toward the novel's final compression.

The novel's terminal hinge episode, matched with 3.11 at total intensity 23: four plotlines converge as Blicero's sacrifice (P02) and Enzian's convergence (P03) reach maximum intensity simultaneously, Slothrop scatters, and the film-body line recurs. The descent into the Orpheus Theatre fuses terminal-rocket-afterlife, corporate-military web, and sexuality-domination at triple maximum—the novel ends not with resolution but with the rocket's parabola frozen above the audience, all plotlines suspended in the instant before impact.
